summary: applyMigrations defined at db.pg.ts:25 but never called anywhere in runtime
details: applyMigrations() signature takes PgClient, resolves migration-pg/ folder, handles both PGlite and node-postgres paths. But neither adapter.ts, bootstrap, nor app-runtime.ts invoke it. PG migrations are dead code unless manually called.

summary: migration-pg/ has 1 migration vs migration/ has 20 — massive gap
details: 20 SQLite migrations exist chronologically. Only 1 PG migration (initial full schema dump). No process to keep PG migrations in sync with schema changes. Missing PG drizzle config.

summary: PG-002: Migration runner plan — 3 blocking safety issues, 5 warnings
details: Safety audit of pg-002-migration-runner.v1.json identified: Blockers: (1) CLI entry leaks PG connections on error — no pool.end() in catch path. (2) PGlite migration path has no transaction wrapping — partial failure corrupts schema. (3) PG schema .pg.sql.ts files re-export sqlite-core definitions, DB:generate:pg would produce wrong DDL. Warnings: (1) getPath() Effect dependency risk acknowledged but underexplained. (2) OPENCODE_DB_DIALECT env var conflicts in naming convention with OPENCODE_DATABASE_* prefix. (3) No credential isolation — process.env read at module level breaks security doctrine. (4) applyMigrations() doesn't close PG pool after success. (5) CLI --db-path flag not handled for PG path.

summary: Misleading variable name 'electronWindows' in titlebar.tsx — no Electron runtime used; Tauri is the desktop runtime
details: The variable 'electronWindows' (titlebar.tsx:81) checks if platform === 'windows' && !tauriApi(), but the name implies Electron-specific behavior. The project uses Tauri, not Electron. The variable should be renamed to 'nativeWindows' or 'desktopWindows' to avoid confusion. Affects 5 references at lines 81, 231, 232, 235, 522.

summary: Tool cache inflight dedup is non-atomic — Ref.get+mutate allows duplicate concurrent computation on same key
details: packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts:100 uses Ref.get(inflight) then mutates the returned Map, then Ref.set. Two fibers can both check, both find no entry, and both compute the same key. Lane AH-001 now fixing with Ref.modify.

summary: Coordination wave_status query uses AND across task_ids — inArray needed
details: packages/opencode/src/tool/coordination.ts:618-622: group.task_ids.map(tid => eq(task_id, tid)) inside and(...) creates WHERE task_id='a' AND task_id='b' which can never match >1 row. Must use inArray(). Lane AH-003 fixing.

summary: Coordination formatStructuredResult emits unescaped XML — prompt injection risk
details: packages/opencode/src/tool/coordination.ts:505: formatStructuredResult interpolates claim.taskId, claim.description, claim.result, claim.error directly into XML tags without escaping <, >, &, ". Malicious or malformed input can produce invalid output or prompt-injection vectors.

summary: Two tools in registry are placeholders (delegate.ts, session-diff.ts) — erodes trust in tool registry
details: delegate.ts (22 lines) records a message string but doesn't spawn subagents. session-diff.ts (22 lines) returns literal {status:"placeholder", note:"not yet implemented"}. Lane AH-006 resolving.

summary: Lifecycle allowedTools is prompt-based only — no actual enforcement at tool execution boundary
details: Phase definitions at packages/opencode/src/lifecycle/definition.ts:16 define allowedTools arrays, but enforcement is delegated to the model's judgment rather than gated at the permission/session execution boundary. Lane AH-005 addressing.

summary: ToolCache.getOrCompute inflight dedup broken — cleanup removes inflight guard before cache is populated, allowing duplicate compute
details: In packages/opencode/src/tool/cache.ts, the getOrCompute function has a race window between Effect.ensuring inflight cleanup and Ref.update(cache) cache insertion. When compute() finishes, the ensuring callback removes the key from the inflight map BEFORE the cache is populated. A concurrent fiber arriving in this window sees: cache miss → inflight miss → starts duplicate compute. Affected lines: ~177-195 (inflight cleanup via ensuring) vs ~198-208 (cache insertion via Ref.update). See full race analysis in cartography artifact.

summary: ToolCache.getOrCompute is defined but never called — dead API surface
details: cache.getOrCompute is defined at cache.ts:30 and implemented at cache.ts:68, but no caller in the entire codebase calls it. The cacheable flag on tool defs (tool.ts:104, registry.ts:528) is only used in session/tools.ts:89 to select a semaphore (readSemaphore vs writeSemaphore), not to route through ToolCache. The ToolCache service is instantiated in registry.ts:142 but only used for cache.stats() at line 541. The only other references are: tool.ts:8 (dead import, never used), registry.ts:105/142/572 (stats only), app-runtime.ts:40/105 (layer provision). 8 tools declare cacheable: true (read.ts, glob.ts, grep.ts, read-artifact.ts, read-source.ts, read-lib.ts, rig-schema-validate.ts, rig-jsonl-query.ts) but none actually use ToolCache.

summary: ToolCache.inflight map mutated via direct reference mutation bypassing Ref.update
details: At cache.ts:108, `const pending = yield* Ref.get(inflight)` obtains a direct reference to the underlying Map, then at line 116 mutates it with `pending.set(key, d)`. This works because Map is a reference type, but it bypasses Ref's immutability contract and makes the inflight dedup fragile. If Ref were to ever return a defensive copy or snapshot, the dedup would silently break. The ensuring callback at line 120 also uses Ref.update with mutation (m.delete(key); return m), which is an inconsistent pattern.

summary: Desktop health check pattern: sidecar health poll via /global/health with 3s timeout
details: Electron main process polls sidecar server at /global/health every 100ms with 3s fetch timeout (packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:204-228). No exponential backoff. Startup has 60s stall timeout for sidecar process. Renderer assumes sidecar health passes before ConnectionGate resolves — UI is blocked until healthy.

summary: verifyUpdateCodeSignature disabled on Windows builds
details: electron-builder.config.ts line 66: `verifyUpdateCodeSignature: false` means auto-updates on Windows skip signature verification. This is a security gap — users could be served unsigned updates without detection. Marked as intentional, but no comment explains why.

summary: No main-process global error handler in Electron app
details: index.ts imports `initCrashReporter` (electron crash reporter) but has no `process.on('uncaughtException')` or `process.on('unhandledRejection')` handler. Sidecar child process has no restart logic if it crashes post-startup. Renderer has `recordFatalRendererError` IPC channel.

summary: Auto-update is gated behind app.isPackaged && CHANNEL !== 'dev'
details: UPDATER_ENABLED constant in constants.ts line 12: `app.isPackaged && CHANNEL !== 'dev'`. This means dev builds never test auto-update. In dev mode, all updater calls silently return `{ updateAvailable: false }`. Potential for breakage between dev and prod builds that goes undetected until release.

summary: Event taxonomy is 90% consistent but eventType is freeform string — no global registry, 7 naming inconsistencies, 60% of canonical events don't exist yet
details: Audited 39 BusEvent.define() registrations across 25 files. 43 unique event type strings found. 7 naming inconsistencies (hyphenated exceptions, overlapping semantics, deprecated-but-published). Only 40% of canonical taxonomy is covered — agent.*, validation.*, checkpoint.*, plugin.*, migration.*, debug.* domains are entirely missing. All payloads have Effect Schema validation but type strings are unvalidated freeform literals. 5-phase migration plan created in docs/json/opencode/findings/event-taxonomy-audit.v1.json

summary: Plugin API has zero sandboxing — 5 critical vulnerabilities: arbitrary file read, prompt exfiltration, secret access, tool impersonation, prompt injection. No capability manifests exist.
details: Plugin security audit (docs/json/opencode/audits/plugin-security-manifest-audit.v1.json) found 11 vulnerabilities (5 CRITICAL, 4 HIGH). Any plugin can: read all project files including .env, exfiltrate every LLM prompt via hooks, access API keys from config, register tools that impersonate built-ins (no namespace enforcement), inject arbitrary content into agent context via system/message transform hooks, bypass permission gates. Plugins run in the same Node.js/Bun process with zero sandboxing. The plugin API was designed for trusted auth providers with no adversarial threat model. Proposed 14-capability manifest schema with runtime enforcement at hook dispatch, tool registration, config exposure, and network access boundaries.

summary: RL-004 secrets policy plan lacks cross-reference to database credential isolation work
details: RL-004 Layer 2 (env blocklist) and Layer 5 (credential lifecycle) overlap with database_security_doctrine.v1.json credential isolation patterns but never reference them. Need to compose Redacted+Effect Config pattern with ENV_BLOCKLIST approach. File: docs/json/opencode/plans/rl-004-secrets-policy.v1.json

summary: Safe/degraded mode is completely absent — sidecar crash, PGlite failure, or migration failure all crash the app outright with no recovery path
details: Safe Mode architecture audit (docs/json/opencode/plans/safe-degraded-mode-init.v1.json) found 9 degradation points. Sidecar is a single point of failure — if the sidecar process crashes or times out, the app shows a generic error dialog and cannot start. PGlite constructor has zero error handling (no try/catch around new PGlite(dataDir)). MCP servers can block app startup if they hang. DuckDB silently degrades (WASM → CLI fallback, no user notification). Plugins have error handling but no quarantine mechanism. Agent loop crashes lose session state with no resume/recovery. Proposed 3-tier architecture (Safe Mode → Degraded Mode → Full Mode) with 6 implementation phases covering ~35 files and ~2,500 new lines.

summary: Secrets handling has 5 critical gaps — sanitizedProcessEnv is a no-op, no output scrubber, no file-read security, no ENV_BLOCKLIST, no bash security
details: Secrets handling audit (docs/json/opencode/secrets/rl-004-findings.v1.json, 332 lines) found 5 critical gaps: (A) sanitizedProcessEnv() does nothing despite its name, (B) no file-read security (no binary detection, safe path, or sensitive file blocking), (C) no bash command security, (D) no general-purpose output scrubber for tool results/agent messages, (E) no centralized ENV_BLOCKLIST. 8 existing redaction mechanisms exist but each is narrowly scoped (HTTP recorder, LLM provider errors, debug config, PG strings, DuckDB firewall, session export, proxy headers). Top active leak site: OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT serializes ALL credentials into a single env var for subprocesses. Proposed 5-layer policy: Detection, Prevention, Scrubbing, Guarding, Integrity.

summary: OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT credential serialization leak fixed via ENV_BLOCKLIST inclusion — explicit opts.env override bypasses blocklist so the workspace adapter still receives credentials
details: The active leak in workspace.ts:570 serializes auth.all() into OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT env var for subprocesses. The fix: add to ENV_BLOCKLIST so it's stripped from ALL default env copies, but survives in opts.env overrides (workspace adapter still gets it). This is the most surgical fix — no refactoring needed.

summary: sanitizedProcessEnv() renamed from no-op to actual filtering via sanitizeEnv delegation — misnamed function finally does what it says
details: packages/core/src/util/opencode-process.ts:19 function sanitizedProcessEnv copies ALL process.env unchanged. The plan replaces its body with `return sanitizeEnv(process.env, overrides)` — a one-line fix that eliminates the most misleading named function in the codebase.

summary: CRITICAL: packages/desktop/src/main/ services use plain async/await, not Effect-TS Service/Layer — zero precedent for Effect dependency injection in this package
details: Deep investigation of packages/desktop/src/main/ revealed that all 9 service modules (18+ async functions across server.ts, updater.ts, logging.ts, sidecar.ts, github-ipc.ts, apps.ts, plugin-transport-ipc.ts, ipc-contract.ts, ipc.ts) use plain async/await. Effect (v1) is only imported in index.ts (3 symbols: Deferred, Effect, Fiber) as an entry point orchestration wrapper. Zero uses of Service, Layer, Context.Tag exist anywhere in this package. The pattern is adapter-based: `Effect.promise(() => asyncServiceFn())`. This is important because it means the desktop-doctor plan's use of async/await with Promise.allSettled follows established convention — not a violation.

summary: packages/desktop/native/ directory does NOT exist — mac_window.node and swift-build/ references in electron-builder config cannot be bundled
details: Desktop Doctor design discovered that packages/desktop/native/ directory is missing from disk. electron-builder.config.ts references mac_window.node and swift-build/ from this directory, but it doesn't exist. Any Bundling health check (DD-01) will fail. This is a packaging gap that explains why native titlebar/drag features may silently fail in production builds. Found during RL-002 Desktop Doctor cartography.

summary: 7-lane session has 7 shared file contention points across campaign/index.ts, runtime-event.ts, registry.ts, plugin/index.ts, state-machine.ts, secretary.ts, binder.ts
details: 7 lanes launched simultaneously with known shared file conflicts that must be consolidated post-completion. Shared files by lane: campaign/index.ts (L1,L5,L7), runtime-event.ts (L1,L5), registry.ts (L1,L2,L6), plugin/index.ts (L3,L4), state-machine.ts (L1,L5,L7), secretary.ts (L1,L5), binder.ts (L1,L7). Consolidation plan created at docs/json/opencode/plans/cross-lane-consolidation.v1.json.

summary: safeInvoke imported at packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts:4 but never called — dead code import
JsonMigration removed from virtual:opencode-server but sidecar.ts still destructures it — TypeError at runtime if needsMigration is true (clean PG install)
FileMemory.isGeneratedFile() and isProtectedFile() use relative-path startsWith() — silently miss absolute paths, no path normalization
DuckDB projection worker sends DDL through read-only subprocess — silent failure
details: See docs/json/cartography/truth_closure_lanes_5_6_7.v1.json for full analysis. 3 issues: (1) -readonly DDL, (2) _ctx_file_relevance name collision, (3) _ctx_agent_heatmap missing table.

summary: duckdb-rank.ts exported functions have zero importers — dead code
details: packages/opencode/src/context/duckdb-rank.ts exports 6 functions (rankWorkingSet, rankWorkingSetFull, queryCochange, queryAgentHeatmap, queryErrorFiles) but has zero importers anywhere in src/ or test/. The file is 237 lines of dead code. Should be removed or resurrected with actual consumers.

summary: duckdb-rank.ts exports 6 functions with zero importers — dead analytical query module
details: duckdb-rank.ts:237 lines, 6 exported functions, zero importers across the whole codebase. These DuckDB analytical rank/heatmap queries are dead code — no consumer calls them. Likely left over from an earlier architecture phase.

summary: fixture/db.ts still references SQLite paths and file artifacts
details: test/fixture/db.ts imports Database from @/storage/db and its resetDatabase() function deletes SQLite-specific files (.db, .db-wal, .db-shm). The app has migrated to PG and the Database API now wraps PGlite/pg, not SQLite. The WAL/SHM cleanup is dead code for the PG path. Recommend updating to use PGlite :memory: or PG truncation instead.

summary: Effect-TS runtime audit complete — 7 critical issues, full report at docs/json/audits/effect-runtime-hunt.v1.json
EVENT_EXPLAIN IPC channel defined with contract but no handler registered
details: IPC.handle.EVENT_EXPLAIN ("opencode:event:explain") is defined in ipc-channels.ts:73 and has a contract entry in ipc-contract.ts:244, but no ipcMain.handle() registration exists. No preload bridge method invokes it, so it cannot be triggered currently, but it represents dead code with a misleading contract entry that will cause a runtime error if any future caller tries to use it.

summary: Campaign predicates.ts: 14 crash-risk [0]! array accesses on filter() results that can return empty arrays
details: packages/opencode/src/campaign/predicates.ts uses `[0]!` on `.filter()` results in 14+ locations. If the filter returns an empty array (e.g., no matching events found), the non-null assertion + `.id` access crashes at runtime. This is the largest single crash surface in the codebase.

summary: Effect.runSync at db.ts module load time crashes on import if RuntimeFlags not ready
details: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts line 22-23: const readRuntimeFlags = () => Effect.runSync(...). This is called as a default parameter to getChannelPath() and Client(), which executes at module import time. If RuntimeFlags.Service is not yet initialized, this crashes any file that imports from db.ts.

summary: Provider languages/sdk Maps grow unboundedly with every resolved model ID — no eviction, never cleared
details: packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts lines 1221,1228: Module-level `languages` and `sdk` Maps retain LanguageModelV3 and BundledSDK instances forever. These wrap HTTP clients, auth tokens, and large SDK objects. No LRU, TTL, or eviction policy. Process-lifetime memory leak.

summary: Explanation event cache Map grows unboundedly — clear/invalidate functions have zero callers
details: packages/opencode/src/event/explanation.ts:50 module-level cache Map accumulates every explained event forever. `clearExplanationCache()` and `invalidateExplanation()` are exported but have no callers anywhere in the codebase.

summary: packages/opencode has 85 TypeScript type errors across 21 source files — blocks typecheck gate
details: `tsgo --noEmit` fails with 85 errors: TS2322 (22), TS2345 (19), TS2554 (11), TS2367 (9), TS2339 (9). Worst files: agent-queries.ts (18), loop.ts (14), explanation.ts (9), packet.ts (6). These are active type failures that break CI if typecheck gates build.

summary: OAuth callback server! non-null assertions in 3 modules create race condition crash risk
details: mcp/oauth-callback.ts (lines 164,168,215), plugin/openai/codex.ts (315,319), plugin/digitalocean.ts (190,194) use module-level `server!` without initialization guards. If callbacks fire before ensureRunning() completes, `server!` crashes with Cannot read properties of undefined.

summary: RPC handler crashes on malformed JSON — listen() and client() parse without try/catch
details: packages/opencode/src/util/rpc.ts lines 7 and 27: Both listen() and client() call JSON.parse(evt.data) without try/catch. Any malformed WebSocket or Worker message kills the RPC handler, crashing the entire RPC subsystem.

summary: ID counter in id/id.ts uses non-atomic counter++ — duplicate IDs under concurrent create() calls
details: packages/opencode/src/id/id.ts lines 19-20: `let counter=0` with `counter++` (read-modify-write). Under concurrent `create()` within same millisecond, two calls can read the same counter value, producing duplicate event/session/message IDs. This causes event ordering corruption and FK violations. Fix: Use `Ref.modify` for atomic increment.

summary: All file writes in filesystem.ts are non-atomic — concurrent writes corrupt data
details: packages/opencode/src/util/filesystem.ts line 60: central `write()` uses direct `writeFile()` without temp+rename pattern. Concurrent writes to the same path produce interleaved/corrupt file content. Every file in the system that uses this write function is at risk.

summary: OAuth callback unprotected global state causes duplicate HTTP servers and race conditions
details: packages/opencode/src/mcp/oauth-callback.ts lines 9-63: Module-level `let server`, `currentPort`, `currentPath` plus two Maps with zero synchronization. Concurrent `ensureRunning()` spawns duplicate HTTP servers. `pendingAuths` Map races on concurrent callback handling causing OAuth failures and security state corruption.

summary: JSONL append writes without file locks — 8+ tools can interleave lines on same output file
details: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool-feedback.ts:88 and 7+ other JSONL append sites use `{ flag: "a" }` without file-level locking. When multiple tools concurrently produce lines to the same JSONL file, lines interleave producing corrupt JSONL output affecting findings, activity, and bash logs.

summary: bus/index.ts uses PubSub.unbounded() everywhere — no backpressure, OOM risk under load
details: packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts line 68: ALL PubSub channels use unbounded queues. Sustained event load produces unlimited memory growth. If producers outpace consumers, this causes OOM crashes. Fix: Use sliding or dropping bounded queues for non-critical channels.

summary: PHANTOM API: 5 watchers call window.api methods that don't exist in Electron preload — silent failure in Electron app
details: 5 watcher files in packages/app/src/ambient/watchers/ call methods on window.api that are NOT part of ElectronAPI type or preload bridge: getGitStatus, getDependencyStatus, getSessionMemory, getTestStatus, getPullRequestStatus. They cast window as unknown to bypass TypeScript checking. In the Electron app, these calls silently return undefined typeof !== 'function' and fall through to HTTP fetch endpoints that may not exist.

summary: No unhandledRejection or uncaughtException handlers — process crashes on any unhandled promise rejection
details: No process.on('unhandledRejection') or process.on('uncaughtException') handlers exist in any production entry point. If any async operation rejects without a catch handler, the Node.js/Bun process terminates with an unhandled rejection warning/fatal error.

summary: GlobalBusEmitter has no 'error' listener — listener throw kills the process
details: Node.js EventEmitter throws on 'error' event if no listener is registered. GlobalBus.emit() can crash the entire process if a subscriber throws synchronously, since the emitter has no error listener.

summary: applyMigrations() never called at startup — DB schema missing on fresh launch
details: db.pg.ts defines applyMigrations() but it's never called during app bootstrap. Users must manually run `bun run db:migrate:pg`. On fresh install, the app launches without tables.

summary: phantom API getGitStatus doesn't exist in preload bridge — git watcher dead in Electron
details: packages/app/src/ambient/watchers/git-status.ts line 12 calls `api.getGitStatus()` but this method is not defined in ElectronAPI type nor exposed in the preload. It silently returns null, making the git watcher always report no status when running in Electron.

summary: 3 missing FK indexes cause full-table scans on workspace, event, account_state queries
details: workspace.project_id FK to project.id has no index. event.aggregate_id FK to event_sequence.aggregate_id has no index. account_state.active_account_id FK to account.id has no index. These cause full-table scans on common join queries.

summary: packages/opencode/src/index.ts MISSING — bun run dev crashes with Cannot find module
details: package.json dev script: `bun run --conditions=browser ./src/index.ts` references a file that doesn't exist. Running bun run dev produces an immediate crash. This is the primary development entry point for the CLI.

summary: 20+ AI SDK provider lazy imports in provider.ts have NO error handling — missing packages crash
details: packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts lines 110-134 define 20+ lazy dynamic imports for AI SDK providers (openai, anthropic, google, etc.) using `.then(m => m.createX)` without `.catch()` handlers. If any provider package is missing or fails to load, the error propagates as an unhandled promise rejection.

summary: Bidirectional dependency: tool/registry.ts ↔ session/{todo,instruction} — fragile init order
details: session/tools.ts imports ToolRegistry from tool/registry.ts. But tool/registry.ts imports Todo and Instruction from session/. This creates a circular dependency across boundary layers that could produce undefined values at runtime if module initialization order shifts.

summary: web-tree-sitter dynamic import in shell.ts:308 has no error handling — WASM binary failures crash
details: packages/opencode/src/tool/shell.ts line 308-321 dynamically imports Parser and 3 language WASM binaries from web-tree-sitter without try/catch. On platforms where WASM is unavailable or binaries can't be downloaded, this crashes the shell tool entirely.

summary: Binary downloads fail on ARM64 Linux/Windows — 5 of 6 tools missing platform entries in manager.ts
details: packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/doctor/manager.ts line 23: PLATFORM_KEY replacement only covers x64 variants. aux binaries (fd, bat, delta, difft, eza) have no arm64-linux or arm64-windows entries. ARM64 users get silent download failures for all auxiliary tools.

summary: Config read pipes to Effect.orDie — any filesystem error crashes the fiber
details: packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts line 391: readConfigFile() uses .pipe(Effect.orDie). Any filesystem error (permissions, corrupt file, missing path) becomes a defect that kills the entire fiber instead of degrading gracefully.

summary: Windows path crash: send-message.ts and read-messages.ts hardcode '/' separator in paths
details: send-message.ts:111 and read-messages.ts:79 use `instance.directory + '/.rig/messages'` producing mixed-separator paths like C:\project/.rig/messages on Windows. Downstream file I/O silently fails.

summary: LSP client registrationListeners not cleaned up on shutdown — minor memory leak
details: The registrationListeners Set in lsp/client.ts accumulates listeners from each waitForRegistrationChange call. When the LSP client shuts down, these listeners are never removed. While each listener is a no-op after its promise settles, over many LSP restart cycles this could accumulate stale closures. Fix: clear the Set in shutdown().

summary: CT-003 surface area map complete — 4 findings, 5 smoking guns
details: Complete event adapter surface area mapped for Lane CT-003. Key artifacts: RuntimeEvent union (15 members, lines 20-37), toSMEvent() (lines 121-155 with 4 collisions), EVENT_NAME_MAP (lines 161-176), toStoreEvent() (lines 179-209), handleRoleOutput (lines 504-513, zero external callers), LANE_STATE_MACHINE (lines 117-212, 15 states, 13 predicate kinds). SG-1 through SG-5 documented above.

summary: Campaign layer (16 files, ~4000+ lines) has zero test coverage — no test directory, no imports from @/campaign in any test file
build.ts reads from deleted migration/ directory — dead code
adapter-dual-write.ts 'sqliteAdapter' naming is misleading — wraps PGlite not SQLite
skipMigrations flag read but never wired — dead config
Slack bot logs raw user messages, full API responses, and startup config to stdout — 3 HIGH-severity data exposure vectors
Enterprise API has permissive CORS (any origin), no user authentication, and trusts x-forwarded-* headers for URL construction
No code coverage configured for any package — zero visibility into test completeness
details: Across all 8 packages with tests (~300 test files), no code coverage tool is configured anywhere. Only desktop has a `test:coverage` script but it's not wired to CI. This is a critical visibility gap for a 25-package monorepo.

summary: Local PGlite does not auto-apply migrations at startup — makeLocalPgAdapter() never calls applyMigrations()
Watchdog port binding race: OS socket not released between sidecar restarts → EADDRINUSE on re-spawn
details: High severity. killSidecar() sends stop message + 6s timeout + child.kill(). On Windows TerminateProcess may not immediately release port. Need: port-is-free probe (net.createServer) before re-spawn, fallback to random port on 3rd+ retry.

summary: checkPgHealth liveness probe (SELECT 1) passes when writes fail — need write-readiness probe
details: Disk-full or read-only filesystem: SELECT 1 succeeds but INSERT fails. Health reports 'healthy' incorrectly. Add checkPgWriteHealth() with BEGIN; INSERT INTO _health_check; ROLLBACK.

summary: HealthRegistry data goes permanently stale — no periodic health re-checks after init
details: Components set health only at init. PG pool exhaustion or DuckDB crash shows 'healthy' forever. Need HealthRegistry.startPeriodicCheck(component, fn, intervalMs) + updatedAt staleness detection.

summary: checkPgHealth runs outside Effect runtime — PG health never reaches HealthRegistry
details: Sidecar calls checkPgHealth before Effect runtime is fully up. Health endpoint shows no PG component data. Need IPC bridge: sidecar sends health-update messages, main process relays to HealthRegistry.

summary: Plugin crash counter lost on app crash — flaky plugin escapes quarantine on restart
details: If plugin crashes app before crash counter is flushed to plugin-health.json, counter resets to 0. Mitigation: fsync after every crash increment, or use append-only JSONL for crash events.

summary: P0: Plugin trigger silently drops unknown hook names with no log — undebuggable
details: At packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:438, when HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP[name] is falsy (hook name not in map), the trigger function returns output immediately with no log, no warning, no telemetry. A plugin adding a new hook name gets it silently dropped. This is the security boundary for hook dispatch — silent failures here are undebuggable. Must add log.warn before return output.

summary: P0: Trigger function uses 5x 'as any' casts on security boundary path
details: The trigger function at index.ts lines 428, 432, 440, 443, 446 uses 'as any' casts on state, hooks elements, hook functions, dispatchGuard, and registration. This defeats TypeScript type safety for the entire plugin security boundary. A runtime type error in any of these casts would be a capability bypass. Replace with typed instanceof/guard checks or extract typed getter functions.

summary: P0: State has both hooks[] and registrations[] — desync risk leads to wrong pluginId association
details: State at index.ts:84-89 maintains both hooks: Hooks[] and registrations: PluginRegistration[] as parallel arrays. trigger() iterates both in lockstep (hook[i] and registration[i]). If they desynchronize during concurrent register/deregister, the wrong plugin's capability check is applied to a hook, or a hook runs under the wrong plugin's identity. Must merge into single array or add runtime assertion.

summary: COND-010: Extract env construction, scanner application, and analytics entry building into named exported functions to enable ≤10-line tests for all 6 integration changes
details: Reasonable-doubt subagent proved that all 6 integration changes (fix-A through fix-G, plus analytics) fail the ≤10-line testability bar because values are constructed inline inside Effect.gen closures. The fix: extract each inline value into a named, publicly exported function with zero behavior change. For example, replace `env: sanitizedProcessEnv()` with `env: getSpawnEnv()` where `export function getSpawnEnv() { return sanitizedProcessEnv() }`. This admits 3-line tests: `expect(getSpawnEnv().OPENAI_API_KEY).toBeUndefined()`. Same pattern for `applySecretScan()` and `buildBashAnalyticsEntry()`. This is purely structural — no behavior changes, no new dependencies, no risk.

summary: P0: Event hooks have zero error isolation — raw fn() can crash event bus fiber
details: At index.ts:369-371 and 377-379, event hook calls use raw fn({ event: input }) without Effect.tryPromise wrapping. The trigger() function properly wraps hook calls in Effect.tryPromise + Effect.exit + crash tracking (lines 445-462). The event dispatch path has NONE of these protections. A single throwing event handler from a plugin crashes the entire event bus fiber. Also: async rejections are NOT caught by Effect.sync and become unhandled promise rejections.

summary: P0: stripSecrets over-redacts on 'key' substring AND is flat-only — nested secrets leak
details: SECRET_PATTERNS at line 181 includes bare 'key' which matches 'publicKey', 'keyboard', 'keyId', 'keystore', 'accessKeyId' — causing false positive redaction of non-secret config that plugins legitimately need. Additionally, stripSecrets is FLAT-ONLY (Object.keys at top level) — deeply nested secrets like config.providers.openai.apiKey pass through unredacted. The plan explicitly specified recursive deep stripping matching redactConfig() behavior; this was never implemented. Compare with debug-packet.ts SENSITIVE_PATTERNS which uses specific variants (api_key, apiKey, api-key) — no bare 'key'.

summary: Pre-existing: skipMigrations flag in makePgAdapter has no effect — migrations still fire even with skipMigrations:true
details: Bisect test expects 0 tables with skipMigrations:true but gets 20 migrated tables. Confirmed pre-existing by running test before and after the idempotency fix — identical failure in both cases. The flag is accepted as a type but not wired in the adapter constructor at packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter.ts:194.

summary: SHOWSTOPPER: appendQueues poison bug blocks reliable JSONL telemetry writes
TOOL-CONTEXT Phase 0: namespace vs toolId key mismatch fragility in modeDescriptions lookup
details: modeDescriptions Map is keyed by namespace (tool filename, e.g. "coordinate") but describeTool looks up by toolId. For default exports these coincide; for named exports they would diverge, causing silent lookup failure. Currently safe because all .opencode/tools use default exports. Fix: use consistent key (namespace) in both set() and get().

summary: tool-telemetry test file incomplete — 1 of 6+ tests written (COND-5 failure)
details: File packages/opencode/test/tool/tool-telemetry.test.ts exists (49 lines) but contains only 1 smoke test. Missing: concurrent invocation test (8 invocations, distinct callIDs), error taxonomy test (5 tagged errors), UnknownError detection test, AppFileSystem fallback test, write resilience test, and success telemetry record structure test.

summary: [P0] MCP process.env wholesale delegation — all host env vars passed to child processes
details: mcp/index.ts:456: ...process.env spread passes ALL host environment variables to every MCP child process. Impact: any MCP server receives AWS keys, GitHub tokens, DB URLs, etc. Fix: pass only mcp.environment + safe allowlist.

summary: [P0] Hook dispatch guard capability enforcement bypass via as-any casts
details: plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts:57-59: as-any casts on CapabilityId values bypass enum type safety. Strings in HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP are cast through any, losing union type narrowing. Fix: use CapabilityId enum directly.

summary: [P0] MCP auth data silently discarded on corruption
details: mcp/auth.ts:62: Schema.decodeUnknownOption wrapped with silent fallback to {} on invalid auth data. Corrupted mcp-auth.json results in ALL tokens lost with no error. Fix: log warning, attempt recovery, never silently discard.

summary: [P0] Plugin identity optional throughout dispatch pipeline
details: plugin/index.ts:82: ListedPlugin = Hooks & { pluginId?: string } — pluginId is optional. All capability checks depend on pluginId for security state. Legacy plugins without id operate without identity-based gating. Fix: require pluginId or assign synthetic id.

summary: [P0] Plugin dynamic import executes arbitrary code without sandbox in host process
details: plugin/loader.ts:141: dynamic import() loads third-party plugin code in the same Node.js process. No Worker isolation, no capability preflight, no sandbox. Module shape typed as Record<string, unknown>. Fix: Worker-based isolation or capability pre-declaration.

summary: MCP module (1053-line index.ts) has zero dedicated test coverage
details: No test file exists for packages/opencode/src/mcp/. Untested paths: connect/disconnect lifecycle, transport creation, OAuth flow, tool list change handler, error handling, process cleanup, status transitions.

summary: MCP notification handler never cleaned up on disconnect (potential memory leak)
details: watch() registers setNotificationHandler but closeClient() never removes it. Handler closure captures State, client, bridge references.

summary: [P0] Empty-string pluginId from resolvePluginId failure contaminates security operations
details: plugin/index.ts:317: Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed('')) creates empty-string pluginId on resolution failure. Multiple plugins share "" identity in capability registry, crash tracking, quarantine. Cross-plugin state contamination. Fix: fail fast — don't register a plugin without a valid identity.

summary: [P0] Positional hooks/registrations array binding enables capability check misattribution
details: plugin/index.ts: hooks[] and registrations[] are parallel arrays bound only by index. Populated at different lifecycle phases. hooks.length drives loop but registrations[i] is optional-accessed. Out-of-sync arrays → capability checks authorize wrong plugin's hooks. Fix: store plugin identity inside the hook object, not in a parallel array.

summary: MCP-TRUTH plan review: 5 blocking, 4 non-blocking findings
details: Full review across 7 axes (coupling 3/5, debuggability 2/5, convergence 4/5, surface area 2/5, testability 3/5, error clarity 2/5, reversibility 3/5). Blockers: (1) 10 Effect.ignore sites vs 4 claimed, (2) no span instrumentation, (3) F4 ACP bridge has no implementation phase, (4) ToolRegistry collision contract undefined, (5) McpFailedError/McpStatusChanged schemas not in Phase 1. Conditions: 6 must-add items before approval.

summary: TRUST-BOUNDARY fix-A: 8 as-any capability bypasses remain unaddressed in plugin/index.ts
details: The plan claims to eliminate 12 as-any casts but only covers 4 (lines 53,57 hook-dispatch-guard + 437,476 index.ts). 8 casts remain at lines 332, 334, 338, 371, 379, 431, 440, 443. Line 332 ('secrets.access' as any) is a direct capability bypass that survives branding. These must be included or explicitly deferred.

summary: TRUST-BOUNDARY fix-G: checkCapability cannot use yield* Effect.logWarning inside Effect.flatMap callback
details: The plan's after code for enforcer.ts:26 shows Effect.logWarning with yield* inside a checkCapability function that uses Effect.flatMap(registry.get(pluginId), (state) => {...}). The (state) => {...} callback is a plain function, not a generator. yield* is only valid in Effect.gen. The function must be refactored to Effect.gen first.

summary: CORONER FATAL: Schema.brand provides ZERO runtime enforcement — all 5 checkCapability call sites bypass via as-any
details: fix-A's Schema.brand('CapabilityId') approach is compile-time only. At runtime, Schema.brand is erased — the branded CapabilityId is indistinguishable from a plain string. All 5 production call sites of checkCapability (enforcer.ts:53, enforcer.ts:57, plugin/index.ts:332, plugin/index.ts:476, tool/registry.ts:423) use `as any` to bypass the current type system and will bypass the brand identically. An attacker-controlled plugin manifest can send any string as a capability and pass all runtime checks. The fix provides zero additional security. Must add runtime validation (Schema.decodeUnknownOption at manifest ingestion, or isValidCapabilityId runtime guard, or tagged wrapper type).

summary: CORONER FATAL: persistCrash/persistSuccess silently swallow I/O errors — quarantine mechanism broken
details: plugin/index.ts:411 and 423 use `Effect.catch(() => Effect.void)` which silently discards every possible error: disk full, permission denied, JSON serialization failure, InstanceState corruption. If persistCrash fails, crash counts are never incremented, and plugins are NEVER auto-quarantined regardless of how many times they crash. The quarantine safety mechanism has a silent failure mode with zero alerting. Must add log.error() to both catch blocks along with retry logic or in-memory fallback counter.

summary: cache.ts getOrCompute interface declares `never` error type but implementation propagates errors via Effect.failCause(exit.cause)
details: Foundation confirmed: cache.ts line 35 declares `compute: () => Effect.Effect<any, never, R>` with `never` error channel. But at line 216, `return yield* Effect.failCause(exit.cause)` explicitly propagates errors. Any error from a tool's execute function — ToolError, ValidationError, TransientError, or defects — emerges from getOrCompute. The only reason this doesn't cause runtime crashes is that executeWithCache uses `as any` everywhere, suppressing type checking. If any code consumes getOrCompute directly and exhaustively handles errors based on the `never` type, it will fail at runtime. Fix: change `never` to `any` in the interface type, or add a proper error type parameter.

summary: classifyError taxonomy gap — 5 of 10 tool error kinds unclassifiable; need expansion for output_too_large, execution_failed, internal, unavailable, dependency_missing, cache_failed
details: Fix-D in the plan addresses this. Current classifyError covers invalid_arguments, timeout, cancelled, permission_denied, tool_error/fatal_tool_error. Missing: output_too_large (TruncateError), execution_failed (ToolError.fatal variant), internal (defects), unavailable (PhaseGateError), dependency_missing (DependencyError), cache_failed (CacheError). All can be detected via _tag pattern matching without new imports (same approach used for PermissionRejectedError).

summary: CRITICAL: annotateToolSpan runs last — all span annotations from cache.ts and normalizeToolError misattributed to parent span instead of 'Tool.execute'
details: Current pipeline order: ...→ normalizeToolError → annotateToolSpan. normalizeToolError taps defects and annotates the *current* span — but the 'Tool.execute' span is created AFTER by annotateToolSpan. All cache.ts annotations (lines 108-150) also target the parent span. Fix: move annotateToolSpan to outermost position (wrap stage 1). fix-I in plan.

summary: Inflight entry removed before Deferred.done — TOCTOU gap allows duplicate computation on concurrent calls
details: In getOrCompute, Effect.ensuring removes inflight entry before Deferred.done resolves it. Concurrent callers between those steps see no inflight entry and create duplicate computation. Fix: move Deferred.done before ensuring. fix-J in plan.

summary: TRUST-BOUNDARY: Public Plugin.checkCapability API bypasses CapabilityId branded type guard
details: Line 487: checkCapability(s.capabilityRegistry, pluginId, capability as any). The isValidCapabilityId guard only protects HookDispatchGuard internal path, not the public Plugin.checkCapability API which accepts `capability: string` and casts to any.

summary: TRUST-BOUNDARY: Zero automated test coverage for all 6 trust-boundary runtime guards
details: isValidCapabilityId, decodeAuthDataPerEntry, persistCrash, persistSuccess, HookDispatchGuard.shouldDispatch, DeviceCodeResponseSchema — all have 0% test coverage. No regression protection.

summary: CapabilityId import as value breaks all plugin tests — type-only export in capability/index.ts
details: Line 38 of src/plugin/index.ts imports CapabilityId without the 'type' keyword, but it's exported as 'type CapabilityId' from capability/index.ts. Runtime error: 'Export named CapabilityId not found in module'. Blocking all plugin test execution.

summary: pg-lifecycle-proof.test.ts: 6 test weaknesses — minimal binder assertions, non-assertive claims test, try/catch ambiguity, no isolation, missing staleness test, inline Blocked/Failed events
